knowledge_base_metadata.knowledge_base_scope: Typecode decoding, electrical data, ordering structure, options, and technical characteristics for FC 300 series and FCD 302
knowledge_base_metadata.coverage_summary: Selection guide provides ordering type code references, electrical data tables, options structure (A, B, C, D), and application/technical descriptions. Full typecode structure details not fully visible in extracted pages.

source_registry.short_title: VLT® AutomationDrive FC 300 series Selection Guide
source_registry.scope_summary: Overview, features, applications, and references to ordering type codes and electrical data sections for FC 300 and FCD 302
notes_relevant_to_decoding_or_datasheet_: Ordering type code sections referenced on pages 59, 61, 68, 74
Options categorized as A options (Fieldbus), B options (Functional extensions), C options (Relay/Motion), D options (24 V backup/RTC)
Multiple enclosure types A, B, C, D, E, F referenced
Electrical data tables referenced for multiple configurations

typecode_values.notes: Decentral drive with IP66 enclosure for multi-motor applications

typecode_values.meaning: Relay card and motion control options
24 V back-up power supply and real-time clock options

typecode_values.notes: Application character 4-6 for A, B and C enclosures
Application character 4-6 for A, B and C enclosures; application character 4-6 for D, E and F enclosures
690 V kW. See manuals for 575 V Hp
Optimized for cabinet mounting, side-by-side installation up to 50 °C
Used for decentral drive FCD 302, suitable for harsh environments and close-to-motor mounting
Used for drives above 90 kW to improve heat management and reduce cooling costs
Three-phase induction motor with copper rotor

typecode_values.meaning: Permanent magnet motor with buried magnets
Permanent magnet motor with surface-mounted magnets

typecode_values.notes: With buried magnets and rotor cage
Automatic tuning of motor parameters without spinning motor
Supports positioning and synchronization without additional hardware
Four built-in PID controllers for process control
Standard safety function compliant with ISO 13849-1 PL d and SIL 2
Supports speed monitoring functions with or without feedback
Monitors motor, load and sensors for predictive maintenance
Supports multiple industrial protocols with dual ports
Enables wireless access via MyDrive® Connect app
Adjusts voltage according to load to reduce energy consumption

technical_tables.table_name: Electrical data – overall power and voltage range
notes: 690 V versions can control motors down to 0.37 kW without step-down transformer
Wide operating temperature range ensures reliability in harsh environments

rows: No cloud required, reduced installation cost, improved system stability documentation
Early fault detection, prevents unplanned downtime
Detection of misalignment, wear and looseness
notes: Complies with ISO 13373 and VDMA 24582 standards
Monitoring adjusted according to system speed

rows: Three-phase induction motor with copper rotor

mapping_rules.mapping_logic_from_documents: Power size code corresponds directly to kW and Hp rating as defined in ordering type code tables
Voltage code (e.g., T5, T7) maps to defined AC supply voltage ranges
Enclosure code defines IP rating and installation environment
Series code determines base product family and capabilities

constraints.exact_rule: Certain power sizes only available in specific enclosure sizes (A/B/C vs D/E/F)
690 V range supports 1.1 kW to 1400 kW
Minimum controllable motor power is 0.37 kW for 690 V versions

family_detection_rules: If typecode contains FC 301 or FC 302 → AutomationDrive
If typecode contains FCD 302 → Decentral Drive
fallback_rules: If value not found in any table or mapping → return NOT FOUND IN THE UPLOADED DOCUMENTS
